title: Tach Guard Next Action
description: The single next required action for an external coding agent (`tach guard next --json`). Instead of inferring session state from several commands, an agent reads one stable answer to "what do I do now?": the `next_action`, the exact `recommended_command` to run, the allow-lists that bound the work, and any live `scope_violations` that must be reverted first. Re-read it after each edit and after each verify.

goal.description: The goal name resolved from the repo's Tachfile.

status.description: An agent-facing status label: editing | verified | finalized | aborted | blocked.

next_action.description: edit_then_verify | fix_scope_violation | run_verify | finalize | done | aborted | blocked.

allowed_files.description: The fs.write globs the agent may edit; edits outside these are rejected at the gate.

allowed_commands.description: The only commands Tach will run, matched verbatim (no globbing).

scope_violations.description: The live out-of-scope writes, if any. A non-empty list means next_action is fix_scope_violation.

path.description: The changed file outside the write scope.

reason.description: Why it is rejected, e.g. "not allowed by fs.write".

allowed.description: The fs.write globs that would have allowed it.

done_condition.description: The exact check the agent must use to decide it is finished; do not claim done until it holds.

recommended_command.description: The exact Tach command to run next (empty when the run is terminal).

instructions.description: Short, ordered guidance lines for the current state.
